publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-29 23:16:21 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, programs, scrapbooks, photographs, notebooks, recordings, miscellany.
did.abstract: Correspondence, programs, scrapbooks, photographs, notebooks, recordings, and miscellany concerning the Cornell University Glee Club, including the complete managerial papers from 1929 to 1980.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Box 26 of Cornell University Media Services Photo Section records, #21-37-2227, for photos of the Glee Club Asia tour in January 1989. Job # 1434, 16 rolls, each with many frames, taken by Gordon Webb.
See also collection #3-13-2843 for a VHS tape of the 1989 Asia Tour.
See also Cornell University Songs and Sheet Music collection, #47-6-2124, http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMA02124.
See also collection, Music performed by students, #37-6-2611 for 1949 recordings. See also the Music Library for many recordings.

label.ref: Subseries D. Glee, Banjo and Mandolin Club Programs

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Glee Club records, #37-6-2399. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Collection includes bound managerial papers, correspondence, and financial papers for every year from 1929 to 1976 (excluding war years), scrapbooks, photographs, programs, notebooks, recordings, songbooks, and miscellany concerning the Cornell University Glee Club (CUGC), its managers, directors, members, and alumni. Also included are materials relating to the Cornell Glee, Banjo, and Mandolin Clubs (predecessor to the Glee Club), the Cornell Musical Clubs Council (CMCC) (the Glee Club's governing body for many years), the Hangovers (a cappella subset), and the Department of Music. Also included are some papers of the Cornell Chorus, or Cornell Women's Glee Club (CWGC).

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also, scrapbooks (1954-55); USSR/England tour (1960-61); publicity and managerial records (1951-65); and an LP album, "Cornell music," consisting of music of the Glee Club, the Cornell chimes, and the Cornell Concert Band. Also microfilmed minutes and a poster for a Glee Club concert in the Philippines.

did.unittitle: Two negative proof sheets of a photo of the Arts Quad showing Boardman Hall and the Clocktower
Marching (Cadet?) Band, 1916-1917 Thirty members plus drum major Robert C. Pooley and George Coleman
Ithaca Band and Orchestra by E. D. Evans Eleven members
Orchestra by E. D. Evans Nine members
Orchestra by Howes (with smaller copy), 1910 Thirty-seven members and George Coleman
Orchestra in Bailey Hall Fifty-four members and George Coleman
Orchestra in Bailey Hall Seventy-one coed members and George Coleman
Orchestra in Bailey Hall by Trevor Teele Seventy-two coed members and George Coleman
Orchestra in Bailey Hall by Trevor Teele Eighty coed members and George Coleman
Orchestra in Bailey Hall, 1940-1941 Seventy coed members and George Coleman
Orchestra photocopy in Bailey Hall, 1940-1941 Seventy coed members and George Coleman
High School Orchestra and Chorus from Tennesee Includes George Coleman as director
Subseries B. Banjo and Mandolin Clubs
Banjo and Mandolin Club of 1890-1891 by Howes, Ithaca, NY, 1891 Fifteen members
Banjo and Mandolin Club of 1892-1893, 1893 Fifteen members
Banjo and Mandolin Club, 1893 Eighteen members (white ties) (from Spencer L. Adams)
Mandolin Club Forty-two members (white ties) and George Coleman
Mandolin Club by Troy (3 copies) Sixty-one members (white ties) and George Coleman
Mandolin Club by Troy Forty-four members and George Coleman
Mandolin Club by Troy, November 6, 1927 Thirty-nine members and George Coleman
Mandolin Club by Troy, 1928-1929 Thirty-eighty members and George Coleman
Mandolin Club (2 copies), 1929-1930 Twenty-eight members and George Coleman
Instrumental Club, 1939 Thirty-two members and George Coleman
Instrumental Club (plus 2 photocopies), 1940-1941 Twenty-seven members and George Coleman
Sage Chapel Choir (2 copies), 1940-1941 Eighty+ members and directors
Scott Tucker's Farewell Reunion Concert Photos
Scott Tucker's Farewell Reunion Concert Photos
Photo of Members with Vienna Choir Boys
Subseries D. Individuals and Small Groups
OctetJ. S. Hower Jr. '28, R. T. Banks Grad, W. R. Morgan '27, H. L. Merriman '28, R. W. Kinne '28, C. E. Wallace '27, N. C. Northup '29, J. B. Moreton '29
OctetW. R. Morgan '27, R. T. Banks Grad, W. C. Sutherland '28, N. C. Northup '29, Chase, Brauner, Young, F. M. Pelton '28
Octet by Troy (3 copies)A. J. McAllister '28, T. K. Powers '30, G. D. Ogden '28, T. Hoffer '28, W. C. Sutherland '28, J. S. Hower Jr. '28, J. B. Moreton '29, F. M. Pelton '28
Glee Club QuintetPiano player, two singers, two banjo players (including Allan H. Treman)
Banjo quartet by Troy (2 copies), Dec. 5, 1926 A. M. Laidlaw '27, C. F. Spindler '27, D. Lewis '28, A. R. VanArsdale '28
Banjo quartet by Troy (2 copies), Dec. 1, 1927 E. R. VanArsdale '28, W. E. Bachman '29, H. S. Brown '29, A. F. Sulla Jr. '29
Banjo quartet by TroyH. S. Brown '29, W. E. Bachman '29, E. R. VanArsdale '28, A. F. Sulla Jr. '29
Banjo player in clown outfit by Troy
Small band (2 copies)Piano, trumpet, saxophone, drums, and George Coleman on violin
B. D. Chamberlain, Leader by Troy
Dexter S. Kimball Jr. '27, Leader by Troy (2 copies)
Truman A. Lacey, President by Troy
Roger W. Kinne '28, Soloist by Troy
Two individuals goofing around by Troy
Glee, Banjo and Mandolin Club by Howes, Ithaca, NY (multiple different), ca. late 1800s (some without director, some with instruments, some with Hollis Dann)
Glee, Banjo and Mandolin Club of 1891-1892 by Howes, Ithaca, NY, 1892 Twenty-one members and Hollis Dann
Combined Clubs by Troy Ninety-eight members (white ties) and George Coleman, Hollis Dann
Combined Clubs One hundred fifteen members (white ties) and George Coleman
Combined Clubs by Troy Ninety-four members and George Coleman, Eric Dudley
Combined Clubs One hundred four members and George Coleman, Eric Dudley
Combined Clubs by Troy (3 copies), Nov. 6, 1927 One hundred four members and George Coleman, Eric Dudley
Combined Clubs by Troy (2 copies), 1928-1929 Ninety-one members and George Coleman, Eric Dudley
Combined Clubs, 1929-1930 Eighty-five members and George Coleman
First Glee Club, Oct. 16, 1869 Beebe, Wilmot, Van Wagenen, Raymond, Ramsey
Second Glee Club, 1880-1881 Seventeen members (white ties and mortarboards); includes list of members
Glee Club, 1893 Twenty-three members (white ties) and Hollis Dann (from Spencer L. Adams)
Glee Club by Troy Fifty-six members (white ties) and Eric Dudley
Glee Club by Troy, Nov. 6, 1927 Fifty-six members and Eric Dudley
Glee Club, 1928-1929 Fifty-six members and Eric Dudley
Glee Club Sixty-six members and Eric Dudley

Cornell Glee Banjo and Mandolin Clubs Scrapbook, 1915-1917 Large photograph of dog labeled "Barnum," group photograph, concert programs, 24th and 25th annual Christmas trip booklets, newspaper clippings (including articles about Hawaiian composer R. Alex Anderson), publicity, etc.
Scrapbook for 62nd Senior Week Show by the Cornell Musical Clubs, 1935 Photographs, concert programs, newspaper clippings, publicity, etc.
Cornell Musical Clubs Scrapbook, 1935-1936 Photographs, concert programs, newspaper clippings, publicity, etc.
Scrapbook for "Here We Are Again" Tour, 1947-1948 Group photograph, photographs, newspaper clippings, press releases, tour booklet, concert programs, miscellaneous papers from 1948.
Scrapbook for Cornell Musical Clubs Christmas Trip, 1937 Newspaper clippings, publicity.
Cornell Musical Clubs Scrapbook, 1940-1941 Newspaper clippings, concert programs (including WHCU dedication), tour booklet.
Scrapbook for "From Blue Cayuga" Spring Tour, 1955 Batavia, Cleveland, Toledo, Milwaukee, Chicago, St. Louis, Cincinnati, Akron.Newspaper clippings, concert programs, tour booklet, etc.
Scrapbook for USSR/England Tour, 1960-1961 Photographs, concert programs, newspaper clippings, press releases, tourist booklets/pamphlets, menus, etc.

Scrapbook for Midwest/Mexico Tour "From Far Above," 1954 Atlanta, Houston, Mexico City, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Denver, Tulsa, Topeka, DavenportPhotographs, concert programs, posters, newspaper clippings, telegram from Kansas governor Edward F. Arn, etc.
Scrapbook for England Tour, 1963 Photographs, concert programs, posters, newspaper clippings, etc.
Victor recording of Alma mater, Crew song, and Cornell medley
Northeast tour, Faith United Church of Christ

did.unittitle: Subseries A. Eric S. Dudley Years
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1923 Also contains "Manual on Musical Clubs Concerts" with instructions for hosting.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1929 Also contains concert programs.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1930 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1931 Also contains 37th annual tour booklet, letter from Romeyn Berry inviting Allan Treman to join the Musical Clubs Council.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1932 Also contains programs for the Women's Glee Club.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1934 Also contains newspaper clippings, drafts of Constitution.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1935 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1936 Also contains newspaper clippings, quartet photograph, letter from Mrs. Roosevelt.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1937 Also contains concert programs, photograph of concert hall.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1939 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1940 Also contains photographs of students in dorm and concert halls.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1941 Also contains newspaper clippings, papers regarding the approaching war and Dudley's approaching retirement.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1942 Also contains papers regarding the politics of the change in directors, the war, and the draft situation.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1943 Also contains agreement by Musical Clubs Council to suspend operations, guide to managing the Glee Club.
Subseries C. Thomas B. Tracy Years
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1946 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, tour booklet, information about the appointment of Tracy as director.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1947 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs (Spring concert dedicated to Eric Dudley).
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1948 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, group photographs.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1949 Also contains newspaper clippings, individual photograph (of soloist?), written Alma Mater sheet music.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1950 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1951 Also contains newspaper clippings, checkbooks from Tompkins County Trust Company, correspondence regarding proposed Brazil tour.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1952 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence (continued), 1953 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1954 Also contains newspaper clippings, arrangements for appearance on Perry Como show.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence (continued), 1954 Also contains newspaper clippings, numerous photographs of the group, individual members, Thomas Tracy, Perry Como and other performers.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1955 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1956 Also contains papers regarding disassociation of Cayuga's Waiters.
Subseries D. Tracy and Sokol Years
Subseries E. Thomas A. Sokol Years
Managerial Forms and Correspondence (continued), 1958 Also contains tour program.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1960 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, papers regarding Soviet Union tour.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence (continued), 1960 Contains papers regarding Soviet Union tour.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence (continued), 1960 Contains papers regarding Soviet Union tour.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1960-1961 Also contains concert programs, papers regarding reorganization as part of university.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence, 1961-1962 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, 1961 Constitution, performance with Nadia Boulanger.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence Vol. 1, 1962-1963 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, Eugene Ormandy performance.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence Vol. 2, 1962-1963 Also contains information about new recording, finances, photographs of album covers.
Managerial Forms and Correspondence Vol. 3, 1962-1963 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
England Tour, June 1963 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Records Vol. 1, 1963-1964 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, second issuing of Constitution, Perkins inauguration, John F. Kennedy Memorial Convocation.
Managerial Records Vol. 2, 1963-1964 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, Robert Palmer world premiere.
Managerial Records Vol. 3, 1963-1964 Also contains newspaper clippings, information about Songs of Cornell recording.
Managerial Records Vol. 4, 1963-1964 Also contains newspaper clippings, information about Songs of Cornell recording.
Managerial Papers, 1964-1965 Also contains newspapers clippings, concert programs, Cornell Centennial, correspondence with Erich Kunzel.

Spring Tour, 1965 Baltimore, Bergen City, Bethlehem, Boston, Buffalo, Darlen, Goucher College, Hanover, Harrisburg, Hartford, Lackawanna, Nassau County, New Haven, Newton College, Philadelphia, Poughkeepsie, Union City, Washington, D.C., Westchester, Wilmington, Wilton High School.
Miscellaneous Papers, 1965 Brief history of CUGC, roster, constitution.
Press Book, 1965 Newspaper clippings, magazine articles.
Publicity Book, 1965 Newspaper clippings, magazine articles, concert programs.
Managerial Papers, 1965-1966 Also contains concert programs, Cornell Latin American Year activities.
Pre-Tour Correspondence for Far East Tour, 1966 Also contains extensive correspondence with U.S. Government and Selective Service, letter from Senator Robert F. Kennedy dated January 4, 1966.
Far East Tour Orientation Book (Vol. I), 1966 Travel brochures, tour booklets, and miscellany about Japan, Ceylon, Malaysia, Singapore, Hong Kong.
Far East Tour Orientation Book (Vol. II), 1966 Travel brochures, tour booklets, and miscellany about Korea and Japan.
Far East Tour News Releases for Each Member, 1966 Brief biography of each participant on tour.
CUGC World Tour - The Daily Journal of Richard H. R. Bull,
A Report of the Far East Tour of the CUGC by Thomas A. Sokol, 1966 Also contains Alumni News article.
A Report of the Far East Tour of the CUGC by Thomas A. Sokol, 1966 Also contains Alumni News article, poster, and publicity from tour.
President's Report, 1965-1966 Also contains performance and repertoire lists from Far East Tour.
Managerial Papers (Book I), 1966-1967 Also contains newspaper clippings, preliminary plans for tour to Canada.
Managerial Papers (Book II), 1966-1967 Also contains concert programs.
Managerial Papers (Book III), 1966-1967 Also contains concert programs, photograph of marquee at InterLakes Finance, 127 West State St.
President's Report - Centennial Year, 1967-1968 Also contains centennial program.
Managerial Papers (Book I), 1967-1968 Also contains newspaper clipping, concert programs, centennial material.
Managerial Papers (Book II), 1967-1968 Also contains concert programs.
Managerial Records (Book I), 1968-1969 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Records (Book II), 1968-1969 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Proposed South America Tour, 1969-1970 Also contains letters from Secretary of State William P. Rogers, Sol M. Linowitz, and Robert W. Purcell.
President's Report, 1969-1970 Also contains informatinon formation of the Glee Club Association, first newsletter.
Managerial Records, 1969-1970 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Association Papers, 1969-1970 Also contains first two issues of newsletter.
Association, Advisory Council, Presidential Papers, 1969-1970 Also contains Association tax exempt papers.

Association, Advisory Council, Presidential Papers, 1970-1971 Also contains information regarding the Gail Singleton audition debacle, letters from Nicholas H. Noyes, letter and sheet music for "The Sea, The Sky, and The Mountains" by R. Alex Anderson.
President's Report, 1970-1971 Also contains information regarding the Gail Singleton audition debacle, newsletters #3 and #4.
Managerial Records - German Tour 1970 (I), 1969-1970 Also contains concert programs.
Managerial Records - German Tour 1970 (II), 1969-1970 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Managerial Papers (Book I), 1970-1971 Also contains newspaper clippings and concert programs.
Managerial Papers (Book II), 1970-1971 Also contains newspaper clippings and concert programs.
Spring "Junket" Book One (Managerial Papers), 1971 Also contains newspaper clippings and concert programs.
Spring "Junket" Book Two (Managerial Papers), 1971 Also contains newspaper clippings and concert programs.
Managerial Papers - Eastern European Tour - General Index and Tour Report,
Managerial Papers (Book I), 1971-1972 Also contains newspaper clippings, recruiting booklet, "RIP Hangovers" paper.
Managerial Papers (Book II), 1971-1972 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Papers (Book III), 1971-1972 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Papers - Eastern European Tour Papers (Book IV), 1971-1972 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Papers - Eastern European Tour Papers (Book V), 1971-1972 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Papers - Eastern European Tour Papers (Book VI), 1971-1972 Also contains correspondence with composer Franz Biebl, correspondence regarding George Gull's stolen camera/film and its return.
Managerial Papers - Eastern European Tour Papers (Book VII), 1971-1972 Also contains newspapers clippings, concert programs.
Miscellaneous, 1972-1973 Press book, concert programs, letter about amendment to Constitution regarding gender, newsletters #7 and #8.
Managerial Papers (Book I, Part I), 1972-1973 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Papers (Book I, Part II), 1972-1973 Also contains newspaper clippings.

Managerial Papers (Book II, Part I), 1972-1973 Also contains newspaper clippings, discussion of moving accounts out of Music Department, press regarding amendment to the Constitution removing the word "male."
Managerial Papers (Book II, Part II), 1972-1973 Also contains newspaper clippings, press book rewrite.

Presidential Correspondence, Advisory Council, Alumni Association/Correspondence, Annual Reports, 1972-1973 Also contains newspaper clippings, newsletters #6-8, endowment drive correspondence, "Song of the Classes" verses (grad student, tax payers, politics), clippings regarding death of Thomas Tracy and memorial endowment, Thomas Swart memorial (non-CUGC alumnus whose death notice directed money to the CUGC).
Southeastern Tour, 1973 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Southeastern Tour (Book #2), January 1973 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Southeastern Tour (Book #3), January 1973 Also contains concert programs, "The Hill" parody, newsletter #8.
Managerial Papers, 1973-1974 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Aborted West Coast Tour - Intended for Winter 1975
Senior Week and Homecoming, 1974 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Records, 1974-1975 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Records (continued), 1974-1975 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs (Commencement, Sage Chapel Centennial).
Managerial Records (continued), 1974-1975 Also contains newspaper clippings.
Southern States Tour, January 1975 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Ithaca Concerts (Homecoming 1975, Senior Week 1976), 1975-1976 Also contains concert program.
Northeastern Tour (Book I), January 1976 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Managerial Papers (Juceam Books, Book I), May 1975-May 1977 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, information on hiring secretaries, arrangements for canceled Hawaii tour.
Managerial Papers (Juceam Books, Book II), May 1975-May 1977 Also contains president's and manager's reports.

Managerial Papers (Juceam Books, Book III), May 1975-May 1977 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs, list of Dudley award recipients from 1964-1976 and detailed information about the origin and purpose of the award.
Annual Report (President's Report and Manager's Report),
Managerial Papers, 1977-1978 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Miscellaneous Papers, 1977-1978 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
President's Books, 1977-1978 Also contains endowment drive, solicitations, and Association correspondence.
Annual Report, 1977-1978 Also contains newsletters #17 and #18.
Ithaca Concerts, 1978-1979 Also contains newspapers articles, concert programs.
England Tour (Book 2), January 1979 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert poster.
England Tour (Book 3), January 1979 Also contains concert programs.
England Tour (Book 4), January 1979 Also contains fundraising information, tour rosters for past international tours from Mexico 1954 on.
Ithaca Concerts, 1979-1980 Also contains newspaper clippings, concert programs.
Annual Report, 1979-1980 Also contains newsletters #21 and #22.
England Tour (Book I), 1982 Also contains concert programs, travel brochures.
England Tour (Book II), 1982 Also contains concert program, travel brochures.
Cornell Women's Glee Club Managerial Forms and Correspondence,
Constitution and Preliminary Papers, 1960-1962 Contains papers pertaining to the reorganization under University control and creation of the Advisory Council.
Magazine Publicity, 1963-1966 Booklet of London arrangements, 1963; Cornell Alumni News, Sep. 1963; Cornell Countryman, May 1964; Cornell Alumni News, Nov. 1966.
International Tour Resources, 1969 Listing of Corporations With Major Divisions Abroad.
"Blending Voices: A Scrapbook of Stories and Pictures from a Group Singer", by Jack Brophy
Songs of Cornell, Published by B. F. Lent.
Songs of Cornell, negative proof sheets for book.
The Principles of a Hang: A Docu-Drama of the FTXXI Weekend
The Hangovers Present: Fall Tonic XVIII
"Blending Voices: A Scrapbook of Stories and Pictures from a Group Singer", by Jack Brophy

did.unittitle: Songs of Cornell memos, correspondence, schedules, proofreading, versions
Songs from the Hill final draft
Songs from the Hill proofs, plates

scopecontent.p: Program for The Women of the University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Ithaca Journal Centerfold describing Glee Club 1960 trip to Russia, Cornell Daily Sun article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Cornell Club letter to invite Cornellians to a Gala Dinner Revolving around their stop in Chicago

did.unittitle: Programs, invitations, Cornell Latin American Year

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitation to a reception from the Ambassator to the United States in Manila in honor of the Cornell Glee Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including an invitation for the Glee Club to sing in the California Capital Rotunda on their 2011 California Tour
